repository_intake_record.name: spring-authorization-server
repository_intake_record.selection_reason: Not in WP2 seed list. Added under 'security infrastructure projects' extension category. Spring Authorization Server is the reference OAuth2/OIDC authorization server for the Spring ecosystem. High-density cryptographic patterns: JWT signing (RSA/EC), PKCE (SHA-256), password encoding (BCrypt), mTLS (X.509), JWK Set management, opaque token storage. Adds: asymmetric key generation, JOSE/JWKS patterns, PKCE code challenge patterns — all absent from apache/shiro batch. High language diversity value (Java).

confirmed_crypto_signals.description: RSAKey.generate() / KeyPairGenerator.getInstance('RSA') — RSA key pair generation for JWK Set
NimbusJwtEncoder / NimbusJwtDecoder usage for OAuth2 token issuance and validation
JWKSource<SecurityContext> — Nimbus JOSE+JWT JWK Set for token signing and verification
PasswordEncoder / BCryptPasswordEncoder via Spring Security — password hashing for client secret and user credentials
PKCE code_challenge_method=S256 — SHA-256 based Proof Key for Code Exchange
X.509 certificate support — x509-certificate-generator sample and mtls token binding
keystore.p12 — PKCS#12 keystore in demo-authorizationserver sample
OAuth2TokenGenerator<T> — pluggable token generation supporting JWT and opaque tokens

suspected_false_positives.reason: Gradle publishing artifact signing (GPG/PGP for Maven artifact release) — not application crypto
Maven repository plugin — 'key' refers to API key for repository publishing
GitHub Actions workflow — 'token' is GITHUB_TOKEN env var, not an OAuth token
CI workflow — 'secret' refers to GitHub Actions secrets, not application secrets
OAuth2Authorization entity — 'token' fields are authorization state columns, analysis needed for crypto content

ingress_boundaries: Token endpoint (/oauth2/token) — receives authorization_code, client_credentials, device_code, refresh_token
Authorization endpoint (/oauth2/authorize) — initiates PKCE / code flow
JWKS endpoint (/oauth2/jwks) — serves public JWK Set for external JWT verification
mTLS endpoint — X.509 client certificate presented at connection layer
egress_boundaries: Issued JWT access tokens signed with RSA/EC private key via NimbusJwtEncoder
Issued ID tokens signed with JWK Set key
Issued opaque tokens (reference tokens) stored as hashed values
transport_security_layers: HTTPS enforced for all authorization server endpoints (Spring Security HttpSecurity)
TLS client authentication for mTLS token binding (X.509)
storage_security_layers: OAuth2Authorization tokens stored in JdbcOAuth2AuthorizationService — token value columns
Client secret stored via PasswordEncoder (BCrypt default)
Redis-based token storage in redis sample — serialization converters
certificate_trust_boundaries: X.509 certificate presented by client for mTLS token binding
JWKS URI — public keys fetched from external IdP for token validation
